title: inboxmover.Promote returns NoOp=true + nil error when destination mkdir FAILS — infrastructure failure reported as success, task stranded in processing/ and skipped by RecoverOrphans while its cycle is active
summary: Verified at inboxmover.go:204-216: os.MkdirAll(destDir) failure → WARN to opts.Stderr (default io.Discard) + best-effort ledger line + return (PromoteResult{NoOp:true}, nil). NoOp=true/nil is the ship.sh-compat 'already done' contract — the caller proceeds as promoted. The file stays in processing/cycle-N/; RecoverOrphans skips the active cycle, so recovery happens a full cycle late at best (duplicated or delayed work).
root_cause: The exit-0 compat contract designed for 'source not found (already moved)' was extended to a could-not-complete infrastructure failure; verdict-style success masks a non-delivery.
fix: Promote returns (res, fmt.Errorf("promote: mkdir %s: %w", destDir, err)) with res.NoOp=false on mkdir failure. Keep NoOp=true strictly for the source-already-moved case. Audit the direct callers (ship phase / cmd inbox-mover) to surface the error into the cycle outcome rather than swallowing exit codes.
acceptance: RED: TestPromote_MkdirFailed_ReturnsError — read-only parent for destDir → err != nil, res.NoOp == false (fails today)
Caller check: ship-phase promotion failure appears in cycle diagnostics, not exit 0
connects_to: inbox-promotion-requires-landed-ship (pending 0.9 — same file/family; land that first or same lane to avoid conflicting edits; this item is the mkdir-failure sibling of the verdict-vs-delivery defect)
